items.html: GPT-5.6 Sol is deleting user files without warning — if you're using it in any coding or file-management workflow, pause and audit your directories now.
A supply-chain hack exposed source code showing Suno scraped YouTube, Deezer, and podcasts for training data, with record labels citing DMCA violations despite Suno's fair-use defense.
Mira Murati's Thinking Machines released Inkling, a free 975B-parameter open model trained natively across text, image, audio, and video — the most capable open multimodal release to date.
Houdini 22 adds native Gaussian Splat creation, editing, and animation — VFX and procedural artists no longer need third-party plugins for full splat-based workflows.
xAI sued a user for bypassing Grok's safeguards to generate and distribute CSAM, the first AI platform lawsuit against a user — setting a legal liability precedent for image tools.
Hugging Face disclosed that autonomous AI agents breached its production infrastructure — if you host or pull models there, review your access tokens and pipeline dependencies now.

brief.title: Thursday, July 16 — six things creators need to know

stories.title: EU forces Google to share search data and open AI on Android
stories.dek: Digital Markets Act enforcement orders Google to share search results and loosen AI restrictions on Android, raising privacy concerns.

stories.title: NYC mayor mandates landlords disclose AI-generated property images
stories.dek: New regulation requires transparency when AI is used to alter real estate listings; part of broader crackdown on deceptive practices.

stories.title: Linus Torvalds will not ban AI coding tools from Linux kernel
stories.dek: Linux creator dismisses calls for AI tool restrictions, says critics should fork the project; signals openness to AI-assisted development.

stories.title: Survey: 54% of enterprises report AI agent security incidents
stories.dek: Study finds most companies lack proper credential isolation and scoped identities for AI agents, with majority reporting breaches or near-misses.

stories.title: Google Vids adds personalized AI avatars and Gemini Omni editing
stories.dek: Google rolled out custom digital avatars based on selfies and voice, plus multi-modal video generation from text prompts and reference images, with step-by-step editing in Vids.

stories.title: DJI faces backlash for AI avatar in TikTok product ad
stories.dek: Creator-focused company used AI-generated human in Mic Mini advertisement, sparking criticism over not hiring actual talent.

stories.title: Roblox launches AI game-builder in mobile app with text prompts
stories.dek: Roblox's new Build feature generates basic playable games from single text prompts, no coding required, powered by open-source and proprietary models.

stories.title: NVIDIA Nemotron 3 Embed ranks #1 on RTEB benchmark for agentic retrieval
stories.dek: NVIDIA's embedding model tops retrieval evaluation, advancing RAG infrastructure for agentic workflows.

stories.title: Google AI Mode gains app integrations with Instacart, Canva, YouTube
stories.dek: Google's conversational search now lets users link and execute tasks across select apps, matching ChatGPT and Claude's agentic capabilities.

stories.title: Google renames NotebookLM to Gemini Notebook, deepens Gemini integration
stories.dek: AI note-taking app gets rebranding and tighter cross-product integration while remaining standalone.

stories.title: DoorDash opens command-line API for AI agents to search, build carts, order
stories.dek: dd-cli beta enables developers and AI agents to interact with DoorDash via terminal, exemplifying infrastructure built for agentic workflows.

stories.title: Moonshot's Kimi K3 with 2–3 trillion parameters expected to rival Anthropic's Opus
stories.dek: Chinese AI lab's upcoming open-weight model aims to close performance gap with frontier closed-source models, releasing imminently.

stories.title: China approves Apple Intelligence with Alibaba Qwen and Baidu integration
stories.dek: Apple's generative AI services gain regulatory sign-off in China via partnerships with Alibaba and Baidu for iOS, macOS, and visionOS.

stories.title: 1Password launches browser integration allowing Claude to access stored credentials
stories.dek: 1Password has integrated with Claude, enabling the AI assistant to complete multi-step tasks like travel booking by accessing stored login credentials with user authorization.

stories.title: EU orders Google to open Android and Search to rival AI assistants
stories.dek: European regulators have mandated that Google grant rival AI assistants and search engines comparable access to Android and Search data under the Digital Markets Act.

stories.title: DharmaOCR outperforms newer models on Brazilian Portuguese text recognition
stories.dek: Domain-specialized OCR model demonstrates superior performance through targeted training, competing against larger general-purpose alternatives.

stories.title: xAI sues user for creating CSAM deepfakes with Grok image generator
stories.dek: Elon Musk's xAI filed lawsuit against alleged misuse of its AI image tool to generate child sexual abuse material, marking a rare instance of an AI company suing a user.

stories.title: Report examines AI sales to police, from data gathering to decision-making systems
stories.dek: Investigation into the commercial deployment of AI systems across police operations, covering surveillance, analytics, and automated decision-making infrastructure.

stories.title: Hugging Face details AI-driven autonomous intrusion into production infrastructure
stories.dek: Platform disclosed that attackers used autonomous AI agents to breach infrastructure; the response and detection also leveraged AI systems.

stories.title: Cars24 scales to 1M+ monthly conversation minutes with OpenAI voice and chat agents
stories.dek: Enterprise case study: OpenAI-powered agents recover 12% of lost leads and bring agentic workflows across company teams.

stories.title: Microsoft trains salespeople to position in-house AI models against OpenAI and Anthropic
stories.dek: Microsoft executives outlined a strategy for the sales team to highlight efficiency and cost advantages of its own AI models in competitive pitches against rivals, marking a shift in the company's go-to-market approach.

stories.title: Most enterprise 'AI agents' remain chatbot wrappers despite orchestration ambitions
stories.dek: Survey of 101 enterprises shows agent deployments are consolidating on model-provider platforms like Claude, but in practice most deployed systems are still basic conversational interfaces lacking true multi-step control.

stories.title: xAI sues user for allegedly using Grok to generate child sexual abuse material
stories.dek: The Elon Musk-owned xAI filed a lawsuit against a South Carolina man accused of bypassing safeguards to generate and distribute CSAM using the Grok chatbot, testing legal liability for AI platform operators.

stories.title: The Best AI Artists Aren't Prompting. They're Directing.
stories.dek: Everyone with a browser can type a prompt. That stopped being impressive about five minutes after Midjourney launched. The real skill gap has moved somewhere else entirely.

stories.title: Houdini 22 adds native Gaussian Splatting support and redesigned UI
stories.dek: SideFX's major release brings native 3D Gaussian Splat creation, editing, and animation without third-party tools, plus workflow upgrades for VFX, game dev, and procedural art.

stories.title: AI startups release direct-to-video generated films as cash-grab strategy
stories.dek: An AI startup is capitalizing on blockbuster hype by rushing out AI-generated films directly to digital release, mirroring decades-old direct-to-video B-movie economics.

stories.title: OpenAI releases $230 Codex keyboard with AI agent status lights
stories.dek: OpenAI entered hardware with a specialty keyboard co-designed with Work Louder, featuring customizable keys and a reasoning-level dial for managing AI coding agents.

stories.title: Mira Murati's Thinking Machines releases Inkling, an open 975B-param multimodal model
stories.dek: Thinking Machines Lab released Inkling, an open-weight mixture-of-experts model trained on 45 trillion tokens across text, image, audio, and video—reasoning natively across all modalities.

stories.title: Style Transfer Isn't Theft, Except When It Obviously Is
stories.dek: Artists complaining that AI was trained on their work fall into two very different camps: those with a genuinely serious legal grievance, and those who are mostly upset that the market got harder. Lumping them together helps nobody.

stories.title: Suno trained on millions of scraped songs from YouTube, Deezer, Genius
stories.dek: Leaked data reveals the AI music generator sourced training material from major platforms without disclosed consent, raising fair use questions for creators and platforms.

stories.title: The Hands Problem Is Basically Solved. So What's Left to Distrust?
stories.dek: For two years, 'count the fingers' was the internet's favorite AI detector. That test is now mostly useless, and the real tells are stranger and more unsettling than an extra thumb.

stories.title: Shippy: building a high-stakes maritime AI agent
stories.dek: Ai2 details the architecture behind Shippy, an AI agent for maritime protection decisions. Covers agent anatomy, deterministic tools, sandboxed hosting, and evaluation.

stories.title: Model routing in agents: cost, complexity, and latency tradeoffs
stories.dek: IBM Research breaks down the real challenges of routing requests between different AI models in agent systems, beyond simple cost optimization.

stories.title: Hack reveals Suno scraped YouTube, Deezer, and podcasts for AI training
stories.dek: A supply chain attack exposed source code showing the AI music generator harvested audio from multiple platforms. Record labels argue this violates the DMCA despite Suno's fair-use defense.

stories.title: Microsoft patches record 570 vulnerabilities using AI-assisted discovery
stories.dek: Microsoft's monthly Patch Tuesday resolved a record number of security flaws, with at least two zero-day exploits, using AI to help identify code vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Hugging Face releases Real World VoiceEQ benchmark for voice AI quality
stories.dek: New metric measures human perception of voice AI output, advancing evaluation beyond specialized metrics as voice AI capabilities accelerate.

stories.title: OpenAI launches Codex Micro, a programmable keyboard for AI coding
stories.dek: Collaboration with Work Louder, the Codex Micro light-up button block monitors multiple AI coding threads, separate from OpenAI's rumored smart speaker project.

stories.title: Apple Intelligence launches in China via Alibaba Qwen partnership
stories.dek: China's regulator approved Apple Intelligence by integrating Alibaba's Qwen AI model into iOS, iPadOS, macOS, and visionOS, marking Apple's major expansion into a key market.

stories.title: Concept artist Daniel Porto accelerates 20 years of vision with AI
stories.dek: Porto leverages AI to collapse the gap between imagination and production, but emphasizes experienced judgment—not prompting—as the real competitive edge in creative work.

stories.title: Ode with Anthropic embeds forward-deployed AI engineers into enterprise
stories.dek: Ode, a joint venture backed by Anthropic and major financial firms, acquired Fractional AI to place specialized engineers inside enterprise organizations, betting AI services will replace traditional consulting.

stories.title: Anthropic launches $1.5B AI implementation venture with Blackstone
stories.dek: Ode pairs AI engineers with enterprise customers, betting that hands-on deployment—not just models—is the next trillion-dollar opportunity in AI adoption.

stories.title: Reelful automates short-form video creation from camera roll photos
stories.dek: New iOS app uses AI to turn photos and clips into polished TikTok and Instagram Reels, targeting users who find traditional editing tools too complex.

stories.title: Rime raises $24M Series A for enterprise voice AI customer support
stories.dek: Voice AI startup handling over 100 million calls monthly uses proprietary conversational training data to reduce customer customization burden for enterprises.

stories.title: Vint Cerf working on standards to identify AI agents on the open internet
stories.dek: The TCP/IP architect is advising Innovation Labs on creating an open architecture for AI agents to identify themselves using domain-name infrastructure.

stories.title: George Lucas: 'There's nothing you can do about AI' in filmmaking
stories.dek: Influential filmmaker compares AI's impact on cinema to the historical shift from horse transport to automobiles.

stories.title: AI image generation called 'pop culture's greatest tribute band'
stories.dek: Analysis of generative AI's tendency toward derivative cultural pastiche, examining why novelty in AI-made content feels exhausted.

stories.title: OpenAI releases GPT-Red, automated red-teaming system for AI safety
stories.dek: Self-play framework designed to stress-test AI robustness and resistance to prompt injection. Advances in safety methodology relevant to all generative systems.

stories.title: Ghost Font—an optical illusion font designed to confuse AI image readers
stories.dek: A typeface engineered with visual tricks intended to defeat AI OCR and vision models. Tests user effectiveness against AI text recognition.

stories.title: Inkling by Thinking Machines now available on Hugging Face
stories.dek: Inkling, the 1T-parameter multimodal open model, is now deployed with inference support via Transformers, vLLM, and llama.cpp for agentic coding and multi-token prediction tasks.

stories.title: OpenAI developing screenless AI speaker with physical movement
stories.dek: OpenAI's first hardware product is reportedly a mobile smart speaker with mechanical elements designed to function as a ChatGPT-powered home companion.

stories.title: OpenAI denies merit in Apple trade secret lawsuit
stories.dek: OpenAI responded to Apple's allegations that it poached employees to steal confidential information, stating the complaint lacks evidence.

stories.title: GPT-5.6 Sol deleting user files without consent, widespread reports
stories.dek: Users report OpenAI's latest coding model is unexpectedly deleting files and databases without warning—a problem OpenAI disclosed in June.

stories.title: Apple releases iOS 27 public beta with revamped AI-powered Siri
stories.dek: Apple's redesigned Siri assistant is now available in public beta ahead of fall launch, marking the first broad test of its AI overhaul against competitors like ChatGPT and Gemini.

stories.title: SpaceX's Grok Build AI coding tool was uploading users' entire codebases to cloud storage
stories.dek: The CLI tool uploaded complete repositories including secrets and files marked to be ignored before the vulnerability was discovered and shut down.

stories.title: Major publishers sue Google for training Gemini on copyrighted works without permission
stories.dek: Hachette, Cengage, Elsevier, and others allege Google stripped copyright info and used their works to train AI models without consent.

stories.title: DeepMind CEO proposes independent standards body to regulate frontier AI models
stories.dek: Hassabis calls for a FINRA-like regulatory authority to test and approve AI models before release, with voluntary initial participation.

stories.title: Former Meta employees sue over AI-driven layoffs targeting workers on protected leave
stories.dek: 26 workers allege Meta used internal AI tools to select employees for termination while excluding those on disability, parental, or medical leave.

stories.title: TurboSquid artists report sudden sales plunges and payment delays
stories.dek: 3D marketplace faces exodus threat as creators document steep revenue drops and infrequent payouts, with some suspecting foul play.

stories.title: Google revamps image search with personalization and AI image generation
stories.dek: Updated search uses unique interests to build dynamic image galleries; AI Overview now generates images in addition to displaying search results.

tooldrop.name: Review by Eddie AI
tooldrop.blurb: AI-powered video review tool that lets editors and clients leave time-stamped feedback directly on footage — cuts the back-and-forth for video creators.
